campus.name: South Valley (Aswan) Campus
campus.description: AASTMT South Valley campus serving Upper Egypt, with focus on archaeology, cultural heritage, and interdisciplinary research.

college.name: College of Archaeology and Cultural Heritage
college.parent_institution: Arab Academy for Science, Technology and Maritime Transport
college.positioning: Specialized academic hub for archaeology and cultural heritage studies in Upper Egypt with strong international partnerships.
college.vision: To create an international hub for archaeology and cultural heritage education and research, supporting preservation, innovation, and sustainable development.
college.mission: To prepare graduates with strong academic, practical, and research foundations in archaeology and cultural heritage through interdisciplinary education aligned with labor market and societal needs.

international_collaborations: University of Louisiana (pending dual degree approval)

quality_assurance.description: Dedicated Quality and Academic Accreditation Unit overseeing continuous improvement, self-evaluation, and readiness for national and international accreditation.

programs.name: Archaeology and Cultural Heritage
specializations: Ancient Near East and South Arabian Archaeology

examples: Aswan Air Dome Site Training with Egyptian-British-Australian Mission
summer_schools: University of Cologne – Aswan Summer School (since 2017)

student_regulations.attendance_policy: Strict attendance required; enforced withdrawal applied for violations.
student_regulations.academic_integrity: Zero tolerance for cheating, falsification, vandalism, or misconduct.
